nameMeanings.name: De
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Sacy
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German, Flemish, Dutch, and Jewish (Ashkenazic): metonymic occupational name, either for a maker of sacks or bags, or for someone who carried sacks, from Middle High German sack, Middle Dutch sack, sac ‘sack’.
German: nickname for a plump person, from Middle High German sac ‘bag, belly’.
German: topographic name from Middle High German sack ‘sack, end of a valley or area of cultivation’.

variants.name: Zacharias
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Erwin, Gerhard, Erna, Hannes, Helmut, Irmgard, Kurt, Lothar, Nikolaus, Rudi, Wolfgang.

German, English, Greek, and Jewish; Czech (Zachariáš) and Slovak (Zachariaš and Zachariáš): from a Biblical personal name, Greek Zacharias, Aramaic and Hebrew Zekharya, composed of the elements zekhar ‘to remember’ + ya ‘God’. This name was borne by a Biblical prophet and in the New Testament by the father of John the Baptist, for which reason it achieved a modest popularity among Gentiles during the Middle Ages. As a surname in Britain, it originated mainly among Nonconformists in Wales and Cornwall in the 17th and 18th centuries. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed cognates from other languages, e.g. Italian Zaccaria , Polish Zachariasz, Slovenian Caharija, and their derivatives. The name Zacharias is also found among Christians in southern India (compare Cherian , Zacharia , and Zachariah ), but since South Indians traditionally do not have hereditary surnames, the southern Indian name was in most cases registered as such only after immigration of its bearers to the US.

variants.name: Sackman
meanings: Jewish (Ashkenazic): elaborated form of   Sack  .
Americanized form of German   Sackmann  .

variants.name: Zack
meanings: Jewish (Ashkenazic): variant of   Sack  .
German: variant of   Zach  .

nameMeanings.name: Isabel
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isabell
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isabelle
meanings: form of   Isabel  , now in frequent use in the English-speaking world.

variants.name: Isobella
meanings: form of   Isabel  , which has been popular in England since the 18th century and has recently become the preferred form of the name.

variants.name: Isobelle
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isobel
meanings: Variant spelling of   Isabel .

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

en.lifeSummary: Isabel De Sacy was born in 1228, in England as the daughter of Emery De Sacy and Agnes. She married Warin Bassingbourne in 1248, in England. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 1 daughter. She died in 1292, in Meldreth, Cambridgeshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 64.

fr.lifeSummary: Isabel De Sacy est née en 1228, à Angleterre, fille d’Emery De Sacy et Agnes. Elle a épousé Warin Bassingbourne en 1248, à Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée en 1292, à Meldreth, Cambridgeshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 64 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Isabel De Sacy wurde 1228 in England, als Tochter von Emery De Sacy und Agnes geboren. Sie hat 1248 in England Warin Bassingbourne geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Sie starb 1292 im Alter von 64 Jahren in Meldreth, Cambridgeshire, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Isabel De Sacy è nata il nel 1228 nel England come figlia di Emery De Sacy e Agnes. Lei sposato Warin Bassingbourne nel 1248, nel England. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 1 figlia. Morì nel 1292, nel Meldreth, Cambridgeshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 64 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Isabel De Sacy(은)는 1228년 영국에서 Emery De Sacy(와)과 Agnes의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 1248년 영국에서 Warin Bassingbourne(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1292년 영국 잉글랜드 캄브리지실르 멜드레스에서 64세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Isabel De Sacy nasceu em 1228, em Inglaterra filha de Emery De Sacy e Agnes. Ela casou-se com Warin Bassingbourne em 1248, em Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 1 filha. Ela faleceu em 1292, em Meldreth, Cambridgeshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 64 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Isabel De Sacy родилась в 1228 г. в местности Англия. Её родителей звали Emery De Sacy и Agnes. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Warin Bassingbourne в 1248 г. в местности Англия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 1 дочь. Она умерла в 1292 г. в местности мелдрет, камбриджешир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 64 лет.
